mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and moderating consumer price inflation signal a healthy demand-supply balance that expands profit margins without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 105 indicates peak capacity utilization, which historically precedes supply bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power beyond a random walk benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above 2.5 percent reflects robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real policy rate environment forces institutional investors to chase yield in equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month when the federal funds rate falls below the 12-month CPI growth rate minus 0.5.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops below CPIAUCSL annualized growth minus 0.5
failure_condition: Equity yields do not increase when the real rate threshold is breached, or forward returns show no significant alpha after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating employment growth signal synchronized sectoral expansion that historically precedes broad-based equity rallies, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: The joint indicator produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns fail to outperform the market average.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail and food services sales exceeding 2 percent signals a demand shock that rapidly deteriorates corporate revenue guidance, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
